text: The burden that Habacuc the prophet saw.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: How long, O Lord, shall I cry, and thou wilt not hear? shall I cry out to thee suffering violence, and thou wilt not save?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Why hast thou shewn me iniquity and grievance, to see rapine and injustice before me? and there is a judgment, but opposition is more powerful.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore the law is torn in pieces, and judgment cometh not to the end: because the wicked prevaileth against the just, therefore wrong judgment goeth forth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold ye among the nations, and see: wonder, and be astonished: for a work is done in your days, which no man will believe when it shall be told.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For behold, I will raise up the Chaldeans, a bitter and swift nation, marching upon the breadth of the earth, to possess the dwelling places that are not their own.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They are dreadful, and terrible: from themselves shall their judgment, and their burden proceed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Their horses are lighter than leopards, and swifter than evening wolves; and their horsemen shall be spread abroad: for their horsemen shall come from afar, they shall fly as an eagle that maketh haste to eat.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They shall all come to the prey, their face is like a burning wind: and they shall gather together captives as the sand.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And their prince shall triumph over kings, and princes shall be his laughingstock: and he shall laugh at every strong hold, and shall cast up a mount, and shall take it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then shall his spirit be changed, and he shall pass, and fall: this is his strength of his god.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Wast thou not from the beginning, O Lord my God, my holy one, and we shall not die? Lord, thou hast appointed him for judgment: and made him strong for correction.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thy eyes are too pure to behold evil, and thou canst not look on iniquity. Why lookest thou upon them that do unjust things, and holdest thy peace when the wicked devoureth the man that is more just than himself?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou wilt make men as the fishes of the sea, and as the creeping things that have no ruler.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He lifted up all them with his hook, he drew them in his drag, and gathered them into his net: for this he will be glad and rejoice.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore will he offer victims to his drag, and he will sacrifice to his net: because through them his portion is made fat, and his meat dainty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For this cause therefore he spreadeth his net, and will not spare continually to slay the nations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will stand upon my watch, and fix my foot upon the tower: and I will watch, to see what will be said to me, and what I may answer to him that reproveth me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord answered me, and said: Write the vision, and make it plain upon tables: that he that readeth it may run over it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For as yet the vision is far off, and it shall appear at the end, and shall not lie: if it make any delay, wait for it: for it shall surely come, and it shall not be slack.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold, he that is unbelieving, his soul shall not be right in himself: but the just shall live in his faith.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And as wine deceiveth him that drinketh it: so shall the proud man be, and he shall not be honoured: who hath enlarged his desire like hell: and is himself like death, and he is never satisfied: but will gather together unto him all nations, and heap together unto him all people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Shall not all these take up a parable against him, and a dark speech concerning him: and it shall be said: Woe to him that heapeth together that which is not his own? how long also doth he load himself with thick clay?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Shall they not rise up suddenly that shall bite thee: and they be stirred up that shall tear thee, and thou shalt be a spoil to them?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Because thou hast spoiled many nations, all that shall be left of the people shall spoil thee: because of men’s blood, and for the iniquity of the land, of the city, and of all that dwell therein.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to him that gathereth together an evil covetousness to his house, that his nest may be on high, and thinketh he may be delivered out of the hand of evil.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou hast devised confusion to thy house, thou hast cut off many people, and thy soul hath sinned.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the stone shall cry out of the wall: and the timber that is between the joints of the building, shall answer.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to him that buildeth a town with blood, and prepareth a city by iniquity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Are not these things from the Lord of hosts? for the people shall labour in a great fire: and the nations in vain, and they shall faint.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the earth shall be filled, that men may know the glory of the Lord, as waters covering the sea.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to him that giveth drink to his friend, and presenteth his gall, and maketh him drunk, that he may behold his nakedness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou art filled with shame instead of glory: drink thou also, and fall fast asleep: the cup of the right hand of the Lord shall compass thee, and shameful vomiting shall be on thy glory.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the iniquity of Libanus shall cover thee, and the ravaging of beasts shall terrify them because of the blood of men, and the iniquity of the land, and of the city, and of all that dwell therein.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: What doth the graven thing avail, because the maker thereof hath graven it, a molten, and a false image? because the forger thereof hath trusted in a thing of his own forging, to make dumb idols.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to him that saith to wood: Awake: to the dumb stone: Arise: can it teach? Behold, it is laid over with gold, and silver, and there is no spirit in the bowels thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the Lord is in his holy temple: let all the earth keep silence before him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A PRAYER OF HABACUC THE PROPHET FOR IGNORANCES.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: O Lord, I have heard thy hearing, and was afraid. O Lord, thy work, in the midst of the years bring it to life: In the midst of the years thou shalt make it known: when thou art angry, thou wilt remember mercy.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: God will come from the south, and the holy one from mount Pharan: His glory covered the heavens, and the earth is full of his praise.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His brightness shall be as the light: horns are in his hands: There is his strength hid:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Death shall go before his face. And the devil shall go forth before his feet.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He stood and measured the earth. He beheld, and melted the nations: and the ancient mountains were crushed to pieces. The hills of the world were bowed down by the journeys of his eternity.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I saw the tents of Ethiopia for their iniquity, the curtains of the land of Madian shall be troubled.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Wast thou angry, O Lord, with the rivers? or was thy wrath upon the rivers? or thy indignation in the sea? Who will ride upon thy horses: and thy chariots are salvation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou wilt surely take up thy bow: according to the oaths which thou hast spoken to the tribes. Thou wilt divide the rivers of the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The mountains saw thee, and were grieved: the great body of waters passed away. The deep put forth its voice: the deep lifted up its hands.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sun and the moon stood still in their habitation, in the light of thy arrows, they shall go in the brightness of thy glittering spear.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In thy anger thou wilt tread the earth under foot: in thy wrath thou wilt astonish the nations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou wentest forth for the salvation of thy people: for salvation with thy Christ. Thou struckest the head of the house of the wicked: thou hast laid bare his foundation even to the neck.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou hast cursed his sceptres, the head of his warriors, them that came out as a whirlwind to scatter me. Their joy was like that of him that devoureth the poor man in secret.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou madest a way in the sea for thy horses, in the mud of many waters.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I have heard and my bowels were troubled: my lips trembled at the voice. Let rottenness enter into my bones, and swarm under me. That I may rest in the day of tribulation: that I may go up to our people that are girded.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the fig tree shall not blossom: and there shall be no spring in the vines. The labour of the olive tree shall fail: and the fields shall yield no food: the flock shall be cut off from the fold, and there shall be no herd in the stalls.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But I will rejoice in the Lord: and I will joy in God my Jesus.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord God is my strength: and he will make my feet like the feet of harts: and he the conqueror will lead me upon my high places singing psalms.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner
